title: How Should You Play Affirm (AFRM) Ahead of Q3 Earnings
text: Affirm Holdings (AFRM) Pre-Earnings Analysis. This analysis examines whether investors should buy Affirm stock before its Q3 fiscal 2024 earnings report on May 8, 2024. Key Bullish Factors: The article highlights several positive elements supporting AFRM. Market tailwinds: As a buy-now-pay-later leader, the company benefits from growing consumer adoption of BNPL services. Strategic partnerships: Collaborations with Walmart, Evolve, and Liberty Travel enhance market reach. Improving financials: The company is approaching sustainable free cash flow generation and adjusted operating income profitability. Volume growth: Rising active merchant numbers and merchandise volumes support expansion. Key Concerns: However, significant headwinds exist. Intensifying competition: Walmart Inc.'s majority-owned fintech startup, One, had reportedly started offering BNPL services recently, directly challenging AFRM's market position. Valuation premium: The stock trades at a 4.19X forward price-to-sales ratio, well above the industry average of 1.30X. Weak earnings expectations: Analysts expect a 70-cent loss per share, with 1.5% wider losses than the prior year. Mixed track record: The company beat earnings estimates only twice in the past four quarters. Bottom Line: The analysis recommends caution: investors may want to wait for a more favorable entry point given unfavorable valuation, rising competition, and low likelihood of earnings surprises.
